revision_summary: Four targeted fixes required: (1) CORRECT the multi_touch_patterns[0].touches[1] entry — 'Online.' at 02:00 is spoken by Bucky (speaker_1), not Kim. Remove it as evidence of how Bucky treats customer inputs or replace with a valid Kim utterance. (2) FLIP the knowledge-gap read at 01:08 — Bucky's primary coaching gap is bridge execution quality, not product ignorance. Bucky correctly names batteries first; the hedge is about generators specifically. Reframe coaching_implication accordingly (clean bridge phrasing, not a product knowledge quiz). (3) FIX the button-up missing-element count — cherry_pick_moments[3] lists five missing elements but coaching_focus[1] says four. Reconcile to five and update both sections. (4) CORRECT the schema reference in cherry_pick_moments[1] — replace 'intros.tesla coaching_notes' with 'context.ion_offerings_beyond_setter_call' for the battery product point, and ensure the coaching alternative in cherry_pick_moments[5] actually demonstrates the objection_inversion flip (not just a curiosity redirect) when citing coaching_philosophy.objection_inversion. Additionally recommended (non-blocking): add missed moment at 02:31 for the Bucky/Lucky name inconsistency in the text; soften 'failure of attention' in coaching_focus[0] to observable-behavior framing; replace 'structurally fragile' with 'elevated no-show risk' in trajectory_verdict.summary.
